title: Uragoga swartzii
intro: Uragoga swartzii, commonly known as the "Blue Passionflower," is a tropical plant species native to the Caribbean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to alleviate anxiety and insomnia due to its sedative and anxiolytic effects, while also exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga swartzii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Uragoga swartzii include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to treat sleep disorders, anxiety, and other conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Uragoga swartzii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Uragoga and is a part of the larger family Annonaceae, which includes over 200 species. The classification of Uragoga swartzii is further divided into the order Magnoliales, a group of flowering plants known for their distinctive flowers and fruits. The family Annonaceae is a part of the larger clade Magnoliidae, which includes a wide range of flowering plants.
